previewItems.biography: Business coach, cleaning business owner, franchisor & entrepreneur, I’m passionate about business & passionate about helping cleaning business owners!

latestPosts.caption:
Being busy is not the goal. Being profitable is the goal!

Being booked up with the wrong clients, at the wrong price, with an exhausted team and no margin is not success. It's just a fast route to burnout.

A good cleaning business should give you income, stability AND some breathing space. Not just a full diary.

Are you stuck in the busy trap? Book a free 30 minute coaching session and let me help you get unstuck

One of the best wins in a cleaning business is when the owner finally stops undercharging!

Not because they suddenly become expensive for the sake of it. But because they understand their costs, their value and what the business actually needs to survive.

That moment changes everything. Confidence grows when the numbers make sense.

Let me help you make sense of yours with a free 30 minute coaching session!

Are you fully booked but still not making enough money?

That is one of the most frustrating places to be.

You're working hard.
Your team is busy.
Clients are booked in.
The diary looks full.

But the profit isn't there.

That usually means the issue isn't effort. It's pricing, structure, systems, or margins.

And all of those can be fixed. Let me help you!

Profit margins. Not the most exciting topic, I know, but it’s one of the most important!

A lot of cleaning business owners are incredibly busy, but when they actually sit down and look at the numbers, the profit just isn’t there. And that’s the problem.

Being fully booked doesn’t automatically mean you’re making good money. If your costs are too high, your prices are too low, or your jobs aren’t running efficiently, you can end up working very hard for very little reward.

So this week, have a proper look at your numbers.

What does each clean actually cost you to deliver?

Wages, travel time, products, admin, insurance, software, holidays, sickness, employer costs, all of it.

Then ask yourself: after everything is paid, what are you actually left with?

Because once you know your margins, you can start making better decisions.

Sometimes small changes to pricing, timings, processes or team efficiency can make a huge difference to your profit.

And if you’d like help working out where your business could be more profitable, you can book a free 30-minute coaching session. Message me and we’ll have a look.

Friday Focus Tip #34: Build a Team That Stays

Staff turnover can kill your business. Every time someone leaves, you lose time, money and momentum.

• Be clear about expectations from day one
• Invest in training 
• Recognise good work 
• Ask for feedback
• Create a path for growth 
• Pay fairly
• Build a team culture where people actually want to come to work

Your team is your business. Treat them like it.

Ready to build a team that's loyal and engaged? Book a free 30-minute coaching session and let's talk about creating a culture where people want to stay.

Cheaper doesn't always get you better clients.

Sometimes it gets you clients who expect the most, complain the quickest and leave the moment someone cheaper comes along!

A profitable cleaning business isn't built on being the cheapest. It's built on being clear, consistent, reliable and priced properly.

You don't need every client. You just need the right ones.

Behind every cleaning business is an owner juggling far more than people realise!

Clients.
Staff.
Messages.
Invoices.
Holidays.
Sickness.
Training.
Complaints.
Supplies.
Pricing.
Profit.

So if you feel tired, it's not because you're not good at business. It's because you're carrying too much without enough systems behind you.

That can change. I can help.

If your cleaning business feels busy but the profit still isn't there, your pricing is the first place to look. It's not enough to cover wages and products. Your price also needs to cover travel, admin, holiday pay, training, management time, cancellations, and profit.

A simple question to ask yourself today: If every client paid this rate, would the business actually work?

If the answer is no, it's time to look at the numbers properly!

Let’s talk about delegation.....

One of the biggest shifts I see in cleaning business owners is when they realise that stepping back doesn’t mean everything falls apart.

It means they finally get some breathing space.

But delegation doesn’t work when you just hand something over and hope for the best. Your team need clear systems, clear standards and clear expectations.

So this week, choose one task you’re still holding onto.

- Write down how it should be done.
- Show someone.
- Train them properly.
- Then give them the space to do it.

It might feel uncomfortable at first, especially if you’re used to being the person who fixes everything.

But if you want a business that can grow without relying on you for every decision, delegation has to become part of the way you work.

And if you’d like help building the systems that make that possible, you can book a free 30-minute coaching session. Message me today.

Friday Focus Tip #33: Master Customer Communication

Your customer just had a bad experience. They're frustrated. How you respond in the next 24 hours will determine if they stay or leave.

• Respond to every message within 24 hours (even if it's just to say you're looking into it)
• Use a friendly, professional tone (not robotic, not overly casual)
• Acknowledge their concern before offering a solution
• Follow up after you've fixed the problem
• Train your team to communicate the same way

The owners with the best reputations aren't perfect. They just communicate well when things go wrong. Your communication style is your brand, so make it count.

I know it’s very easy to say “have a rest” on bank holiday Monday, but much harder to actually do it when you run a cleaning business!

There’s always something, isn’t there?

A message you could reply to.
A rota you could tweak.
A client query you could sort.
A bit of admin you could “just quickly” do.

But honestly, if you’ve got the chance to switch off today, take it.

Not because you’re lazy.
Not because you don’t care.
But because you’re human and you can’t keep running on empty forever.

The business will still be there tomorrow. And most of the things that feel urgent today can probably wait until tomorrow. 

So enjoy the bank holiday if you can.

Have a slow morning. Go for a walk. Sit in the garden. See family. Do absolutely nothing for a few hours if that’s what you need.

And if part of you feels guilty for resting, that’s probably a sign you need it even more.

If you’re fed up of feeling like the business only works when you’re constantly available, I can help you look at what needs to change. You can book a free 30-minute coaching session or message me anytime.

Friday Focus Tip #32: Stop Underpricing Your Services

Most cleaning business owners underprice because they're afraid. Afraid of losing customers. Afraid they're not "worth it." Afraid of what competitors charge.

But customers don't just buy on price. They buy on trust, reliability and perceived value.

The cleaning business owners charging premium prices aren't better cleaners. They're just more confident about what they offer.

How to price right:

• Know your true costs
• Add a healthy profit margin 
• Price based on value, not just time
• Test a price increase on new customers first
• Communicate your value clearly in your marketing

Raise your prices. Invest in your business. Deliver better service. Everyone wins.

Ready to get your pricing right? Book a free 30-minute session and let's make sure you're charging what you're worth.
